name: Russia–Ukraine
summary_short: A long-standing conflict between Russia and Ukraine that escalated into a full-scale invasion in 2022.
summary_long: The conflict began in 2014 with the annexation of Crimea and fighting in the Donbas. In 2022, Russia launched a large-scale invasion, leading to massive humanitarian impacts, international sanctions, and regional destabilization. Combat continues primarily in eastern and southern Ukraine, while the international community provides humanitarian, economic, and military support.

timeline.title: Full-Scale Invasion Launched
timeline.description: Russia initiated a massive military aggression against Ukraine from multiple axes, including a direct strike toward Kyiv.

timeline.title: Victory in the Battle of Kyiv
timeline.description: Russian forces withdrew from northern Ukraine after failing to seize the capital. Discovery of atrocities in Bucha.

timeline.title: Kharkiv Counter-Offensive
timeline.description: A lightning Ukrainian operation liberated thousands of square kilometers in the northeast of the country.

timeline.title: Fall of Bakhmut
timeline.description: The end of the longest and bloodiest battle of the war; the city was totally destroyed and occupied by Russian forces after months of fighting.

timeline.title: Kakhovka Dam Destruction
timeline.description: A massive ecological and humanitarian disaster following the dam breach, flooding vast territories in the south.

timeline.title: Fall of Avdiivka
timeline.description: Strategic Russian victory in the Donbas after months of intense assaults; Ukrainian forces withdrew to a new defensive line.

timeline.title: Incursion into Kursk Region
timeline.description: A surprise Ukrainian operation on Russian sovereign territory, leading to the occupation of hundreds of square kilometers in the borderlands.

timeline.title: Escalation & Technological Warfare
timeline.description: Massive deployment of autonomous drones and precision strikes on energy infrastructure on both sides.

economy.impact: The conflict affects global energy prices, trade flows, and causes extensive damage to critical infrastructure.

name: Israel–Iran
summary_short: Tensions between Israel and Iran manifesting in airstrikes, retaliations, and high-level diplomatic warnings.
summary_long: Israel and Iran have long competed for regional influence. Recent months have seen a series of direct airstrikes, cyber incidents, and diplomatic escalations. The conflict impacts regional stability, international trade, and civilian populations, particularly in areas bordering the conflict zones.

timeline.title: Operation Iron Shield: First Direct Encounter
timeline.description: Iran launched hundreds of drones at Israel. For the first time, the US, UK, and Jordan directly engaged in defense, intercepting 99% of threats alongside the Iron Dome.

timeline.title: Tehran Assassination & Mobilization
timeline.description: The killing of Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh in the heart of Iran triggered total regional mobilization; the US deployed reinforcements to prevent an immediate all-out war.

timeline.title: Days of Repentance & US THAAD Shield
timeline.description: Israeli airstrikes crippled Iranian missile production. The US deployed the THAAD system with American crews to Israel, marking direct US involvement on the ground.

timeline.title: Cyber Warfare & 'Middle East NATO'
timeline.description: Massive cyberattacks paralyzed Iran's power grid and Israel's financial systems. The US formed a regional coalition with Arab states as a bulwark against Iranian ballistic threats.

timeline.title: Battle for the Red Sea & Blockade
timeline.description: US and British forces struck Houthi positions in Yemen. The US 5th Fleet initiated aggressive naval patrols to intercept Iranian weapon shipments to proxy groups.

timeline.title: Strikes on Parchin Nuclear Facilities
timeline.description: Targeted Israeli operations destroyed key research facilities. The Iranian nuclear program was reportedly set back by years, leading to further naval escalations in the Strait of Hormuz.

timeline.title: Global Standoff
timeline.description: The US maintains two Carrier Strike Groups in the region. The conflict teeters on the edge of a wider war as fighting shifts into high-intensity proxy conflicts in Lebanon and Syria.

economy.impact: Tensions affect oil prices, aviation logistics, and critical regional trade routes.
